2606.07271.title: Where Rectified Flows Leak: Characterising Membership Signals Along the Interpolation Path
2606.07271.paper_summary: Understanding what generative models retain from training data remains challenging, with implications for copyright and privacy. Beyond verbatim reproduction, models can encode subtler traces of their training data that never surface in their outputs yet remain exploitable. We study this regime for Rectified Flows, which are increasingly used in deployed generative systems. We analyse the interpolation path $X_λ= (1-λ)X_0 + λX_1$ that defines the Rectified Flow training. We show that a gap exists between the reconstruction of train and test data that follows a bell-shaped curve over $λ$, wich accumulates during training, while the validation metrics remain stable. The signal has a maximum whose location we derive in closed form under Gaussian assumptions. We validate these predictions on both audio and images and show that the bell-shaped structure is universal, while the peak prediction holds when our assumptions are satisfied. As a proof of concept, we exploit this specific $λ$-resolved structure to perform a Membership Inference Attack, distinguishing members of the training set from non-members.
2606.07271.paper_summary_zh: 理解生成模型從訓練數據中保留了什麼仍然具有挑戰性，這對版權和隱私有影響。超越逐字複製，模型可以編碼其訓練數據的更微妙痕跡，這些痕跡在其輸出中從未顯現卻仍然可被利用。我們研究這種情況下的修正流，這在部署的生成系統中越來越常用。我們分析定義修正流訓練的插值路徑 $X_λ= (1-λ)X_0 + λX_1$。我們顯示訓練數據和測試數據的重建之間存在一個差距，該差距隨著 $λ$ 形成鐘形曲線，並在訓練過程中累積，而驗證指標保持穩定。信號具有一個最大值，我們在高斯假設下以閉合形式推導其位置。我們在音頻和圖像上驗證這些預測，並顯示鐘形結構是普遍存在的，而當我們的假設成立時，峰值預測也成立。作為概念驗證，我們利用這一特定的 $λ$-解析結構執行會員推斷攻擊，區分訓練集的成員和非成員。
2606.07271.author: Thomas Sesmat et.al.
2606.07271.authors: Thomas Sesmat, Gabriel Meseguer-Brocal, Geoffroy Peeters
